issues.title: Layer-level index.ts files missing across multiple features
issues.description: 43 feature layer directories (application/, domain/, infrastructure/, presentation/) lack index.ts barrel exports. The architecture rule requires each layer to export a public API. Affected apps: admin, auth, landing, payments, studio (store is better covered). Without barrel exports, internal paths are exposed and refactoring breaks consumers.
issues.rule: Barrel Exports - Each layer should have index.ts exporting public API
issues.fix: Add index.ts to each layer directory re-exporting the public API. Start with the most-used layers (application, presentation) in admin and payments.

issues.title: Hardcoded Supabase local URL fallback in production config
issues.description: apps/admin/src/shared/infrastructure/config/environment.ts defines a hardcoded fallback URL 'http://127.0.0.1:54321' for NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL. This is a development-only value that should not live in shared infrastructure config. If NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL is unset in production, the app silently falls back to a non-working local URL with no error.
issues.rule: No Hardcoding - Hardcoded config URLs must be in env/config, fallbacks to localhost are risky
issues.fix: Remove the hardcoded fallback or move it to a clearly marked development-only guard. Consider throwing an error if the env var is missing in non-development environments.

issues.title: Inline object type literals in mutationFn arguments (payments)
issues.description: apps/payments/src/features/payment-methods/application/hooks/usePaymentMethodMutations.ts uses inline anonymous type literals for mutationFn parameters ({ sellerId: string; nameEn: string; nameEs?: string } and { id: string; patch: Partial<...> }). These types belong in the feature's domain/types.ts.
issues.rule: No Hardcoding - Inline Types: Complex types must be in domain/types
issues.fix: Extract these parameter shapes into named interfaces in apps/payments/src/features/payment-methods/domain/types.ts, e.g. CreatePaymentMethodParams and UpdatePaymentMethodParams.

issues.title: Internal helper component with inline type in DisplayBlockRenderer
issues.description: apps/payments/src/features/checkout/presentation/components/DisplayBlockRenderer.tsx defines the internal BlockContent component with an inline type literal ({ block: DisplayBlock; locale: string }). Though minor (2 props), it breaks the one-component-per-file rule and uses inline types.
issues.rule: No Hardcoding - Inline types in component signatures
issues.fix: Define a BlockContentProps interface at the top of the file (or in a .types.ts file). The BlockContent internal component is acceptable in-file per the compound component exception, but the type should be named.

issues.title: Debounce pattern using local useState + useEffect is correct but repetitive
issues.description: Both apps/admin/src/features/users/presentation/pages/UsersPageContent.tsx and apps/studio/src/features/products/presentation/components/ProductFilters.tsx implement the identical debounced-search pattern: local useState for the input value + useEffect to push to nuqs params after a delay. This is the correct pattern per the URL State rule, but the debounce logic is duplicated. A shared useDebounced hook in the shared package could eliminate this duplication.
issues.rule: DRY - Same pattern repeated across 2+ features
issues.fix: Consider extracting a useDebouncedParam hook into packages/shared/src/hooks/ that accepts (value, delay, setter) and handles the debounce + sync pattern. KISS caveat: only worthwhile if this pattern will appear in a third location.

issues.title: YouTube embed base URL hardcoded in domain logic
issues.description: apps/payments/src/features/payment-methods/domain/youtubeEmbed.ts hardcodes the YouTube embed URL base string 'https://www.youtube.com/embed/' in three separate return statements. While technically a domain utility (not app config), the repeated URL string could be extracted to a named constant within the same file.
issues.rule: No Hardcoding - Repeated magic strings should be constants; DRY
issues.fix: Add a module-level constant: const YOUTUBE_EMBED_BASE = 'https://www.youtube.com/embed/'; and use it in all three return statements.

issues.title: All app/ route files are correctly thin wrappers
issues.description: All sampled page.tsx files (admin, store, payments, studio) follow the thin-wrapper pattern: they only call setRequestLocale and render the feature page component. No business logic or data fetching in route files. This is excellent compliance.
issues.rule: App Router Thin Wrappers - Route files should only import and render feature pages
issues.fix: No action needed. Existing pattern is correct.

patterns.name: Consistent feature-level barrel exports (index.ts)
patterns.description: All features have a top-level index.ts (e.g. apps/admin/src/features/audit/index.ts). The missing barrel exports are at the sub-layer level (application/, presentation/), not at the feature root level.
patterns.suggestion: Continue ensuring all new features have both a root index.ts AND layer-level index.ts files.

patterns.name: nuqs usage for URL state in all filter/pagination components
patterns.description: All sampled filter, search, and pagination components (ProductFilters, UsersPageContent, ProductCatalogPage, AuditLogPageContent, ReceivedOrdersPageContent) correctly use useQueryStates/useQueryState from nuqs for URL-persisted state.

patterns.name: Layer dependency direction is correct
patterns.description: No violations found where domain imports from application/infrastructure, or application imports from presentation. The dependency flow (Presentation → Application → Domain ← Infrastructure) is respected throughout all sampled features.

patterns.name: Presentation layer imports from shared/infrastructure are widespread
patterns.description: Presentation components across all apps import from @/shared/infrastructure/config (appUrls) and @/shared/infrastructure/i18n (Link, useRouter). This is technically an upward dependency but is accepted by the architecture because these are app-level shared infrastructure utilities (not cross-feature), making this a recognized pattern rather than a violation.
patterns.suggestion: Document this as an accepted exception: presentation components may import from @/shared/infrastructure/config and @/shared/infrastructure/i18n for cross-cutting concerns.
